steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a similarity between the two specimens. That means the answer must compare Sue and Thomas directly, highlighting something they share — not describe just one of them or contrast them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Sue and Thomas are both members of the genus Tyrannosaurus': Directly states what the two specimens have in common. Emphasizes the shared genus. ✓
  • Choice B — 'While Sue is housed at the Field Museum... Thomas is housed at the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County': Uses "while" to contrast their locations — emphasizes a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The Field Museum of Natural History... is located in Chicago, Illinois': Only describes Sue's museum. No comparison with Thomas at all. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Thomas is the nickname of a Tyrannosaurus fossil... in Los Angeles, California': Only describes Thomas. No comparison with Sue. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
